rule.statement: End-to-end pipeline (STT+LLM+TTS+tool) MUST stay under 800ms p95; anything above 1.5s feels broken.
rule.rationale: Voice users tolerate ≤1s of silence before perceiving disconnect.

rule.statement: LLM responses MUST be 1-3 sentences; system prompt MUST explicitly forbid markdown.
rule.rationale: Long responses inflate TTS latency; markdown reads as literal characters.

rule.statement: Barge-in MUST be enabled: user speech during agent TTS interrupts immediately.
rule.rationale: Without barge-in, the agent feels robotic and disrespectful.

rule.statement: Tool calls MUST be preceded by a filler phrase ('Let me check that for you').
rule.rationale: Silence > 1s reads as disconnection; filler phrases preserve continuity.

rule.statement: VAD endpointing_delay MUST be 400-600ms to prevent false-positive turn completion from background noise.
rule.rationale: Too short fires on coughs; too long delays response.

rule.statement: Conversation timeout MUST be set (15-20 min) to cap runaway billing.
rule.rationale: Telephony is billed per minute; unbounded sessions burn budget.

rule.statement: Per-call transcript + per-component p95 latency + escalation rate MUST be logged.
rule.rationale: Without metrics, quality regressions surface only through user complaints.

rule.statement: Outbound campaigns MUST collect TCPA (US) / GDPR (EU) consent before autodial.
rule.rationale: Fines are $500-1500/call (TCPA); GDPR exposure is uncapped.
